title: Figma now has AI motion graphics and shader tools
slides: Can Figma accelerate your motion design workflow?
It now generates motion graphics from static layers.
But its AI shaders create effects no one hand-codes.
Your prototypes gain real-time 3D-like motion instantly.
Test one component with auto-motion today, not tomorrow.
thread_text:
1. What if AI did your motion?

Figma now turns static layers into motion graphics — without plugins.

2. Shaders no one writes by hand.

Its AI generates visual effects that human coders skip because they're too complex.

3. Real-time 3D from static frames.

Your prototypes get instant depth and movement — no timeline scrubbing.

4. Auto-motion is already live.

You can test one component with one click and see animation appear.

5. Try it on a real project today.

Open a static prototype and apply auto-motion to one button. Does it save time or add friction?
video_script: Figma adds AI motion from static layers.
Its shaders create effects no one writes.
Prototypes move like 3D in real time.
Test auto-motion on one component now.
Try it today and judge the results.

script: Figma adds AI motion from static layers.
Its shaders create effects no one writes.
Prototypes move like 3D in real time.
Test auto-motion on one component now.
Try it today and judge the results.

instagram.caption:
Your static layers now animate on their own in Figma 🎬

No plugins. No timeline scrubbing. The AI generates motion graphics from flat design files and writes shader effects too complex for humans to code by hand.

Your prototypes gain instant depth and real-time 3D-like motion without any manual animation work.

Test one component with auto-motion today. Does it cut your delivery time, or does the automation miss your creative intent?
